info.title: Sling API
info.description: Welcome to the Sling API Documentation. Sling is a tool that allows you to schedule and communicate with your employees. In order to interact with the API you need to have an authorization token. You can find your authorization token in the request headers sent by any respectable Sling client.

post.summary: Create new shift(s).
post.description:
The shift is not published, it is in planning mode.
We support both timezone aware and unaware timestamps for dtstart and dtend
but obviously not in a single request. dtstart and dtend must either both be
timezone aware or both be timezone unaware.

200.description: Returns found conflicts in dry run mode

201.description: Shift created, returns a list of shift objects that were created

204.description: No conflicts in dry run mode

409.description: Conflict detected, use ?ignoreConflicts=true to override

conflicts.description: Existing shifts that conflict with the desired new shifts

restBreakConflicts.description: Existing shifts that conflict with the desired new shifts due to rest break threshold being exceeded

restTimeMinutes.description: Number of minutes between new shift and existing shift that caused the conflict

existingEvent.description: Existing event that caused the conflict

noShows.description: total number of shifts marked as a no show

nonHolidayHours.description: total number of hours not considered as holiday

salaryCost.description: total salary cost, excludes hourly cost

sickCallouts.description: total number of shifts marked as a sick callout

totalOvertimeCost.description: total cost for overtime, including holiday overtime

totalOvertimeHours.description: total overtime hours, including holiday overtime

hourlyCostByUserId.description: Breakdown of total hourly cost by user. Key is user ID and value is total hourly cost for that user.

hourlyCostByDate.description: Breakdown of total hourly cost by date. Key is date and value is total hourly cost for that date.

hoursByUserId.description: Breakdown of total hours by user. Key is user ID and value is total hours.

taskInstancesToCopy.description:
A list of taskInstance IDs which should be copied to the new shift.
When provided, these tasks will replace the ones that would normally
be created from the associated task templates.

copyTaskInstancesFromShift.description:
The ID of a single shift from which task instances will be copied.
When provided, these tasks will replace the ones that would normally
be created from the associated task templates. Note that if both
`copyTaskInstancesFromShift` and `taskInstancesToCopy` are provided
in the payload, `taskInstancesToCopy` will be silently ignored.

If true, tasks will be copied from the existing shifts. Tasks copied

Optional - Penalty factor of labor cost for the objective function (default 1)
The higher the value, the more important it is to minimize labor cost while
auto-assigning shifts. When 0 is passed then the cost of labor will have
no influence on the result.

unequalSchedulePenalty.description:
Optional - Penalty factor of unequal distribution of hours for the objective function (default 100)
The higher the value, the more important it is to try to create an equal distribution
of hours accross employees while auto-assigning shifts. When 0 is passed then equal distribution
of hours has no influence on the result
